forbiddenClaims: that a paywalled or draft route was inspected or is a live published article
reproduction of the source site's proprietary copy, masthead, exact palette, unique illustrations, photography, or distinctive typography
an article with stripped or fabricated provenance (missing byline, publish date, or source attribution)
a soft login wall that implies a blocked article is live and merely gated
an invented article, fabricated byline, or speculative related-content set not present in the snapshot
affiliation with any real publisher, masthead, syndication service, or wire service observed or implied by the source

rubricAnchors.score1: The adaptation strips provenance, presents a blocked route as live, or copies the source masthead/photography.
rubricAnchors.score3: Provenance is present but partial (e.g., date without source), or a blocked route is shown behind a soft wall implying it is live.
rubricAnchors.score5: Every adapted article carries clear byline, date, and source traced to an inspected route, and blocked routes are explicitly marked not-published.

rubricAnchors.score1: Content-home and article-route share no common article-card, byline, or provenance model.
rubricAnchors.score3: Some shared tokens but the byline/date treatment drifts between feed cards and the article route.
rubricAnchors.score5: A single direction system unifies content-home and article-route, including a consistent article-card hierarchy, byline/date anchoring, and related-content treatment.

rubricAnchors.score1: No actionable blueprint; engineers cannot infer the provenance-visible, related-content-shown, route-blocked-paywalled, or route-not-found states.
rubricAnchors.score3: Partial blueprints with unresolved route-blocked-paywalled or route-not-found states.
rubricAnchors.score5: Engineers can build content-home and article-route directly, including all declared states and the byline/date/source anchoring rule at every breakpoint.

rubricAnchors.score1: A verbatim copy of the source site's copy, masthead, palette, photography, or distinctive typography.
rubricAnchors.score3: Generic principles adapted but a proprietary identifier (masthead, photo, typeface) slips through.
rubricAnchors.score5: Only generic structural principles are retained or adapted; proprietary copy, masthead, palette, photography, and typography are explicitly rejected with recorded decisions.

rubricAnchors.score1: No accessibility handling and no feed-load-error, route-blocked-paywalled, or route-not-found states.
rubricAnchors.score3: Baseline contrast and focus with partial handling of the feed-load-error and route-blocked-paywalled states.
rubricAnchors.score5: WCAG-aligned contrast and focus across both screens, with robust feed-load-error, route-blocked-paywalled (clear not-published messaging), and route-not-found states that preserve provenance and the inspected-route boundary.

rubricAnchors.score1: Claims rely on a paywalled/draft route, a fabricated byline, or an invented article not in the snapshot.
rubricAnchors.score3: Most claims trace to inspected routes but one claim overreaches into a blocked route or invents a byline.
rubricAnchors.score5: Every claim traces to an inspected route, provenance is preserved and traceable, and paywalled/draft routes are explicitly excluded.

adjudicationNotes: Content provenance is load-bearing: stripping byline/date/source, or fabricating provenance, fails evidence-discipline and product-appropriateness.
A blocked (paywalled or draft) route MUST be treated as not-published; presenting it as a live gated article fails evidence-discipline.
Photography and distinctive typography count as proprietary identifiers and must be rejected under originality.
retain/adapt/reject lanes are all permitted: generic content-structure principles may be retained or adapted, while proprietary identifiers must be rejected.

The brief's sourceSnapshotRef.sha256 binds the exact on-disk snapshot bytes; the manifest generator verifies this binding on every build (placeholder sha will be replaced once the real snapshot is authored).
